result: **Goal: Add a "Reaction-Diffusion Texture Generator" simulation mode** that implements the Gray-Scott model — producing the mesmerizing, organic patterns (spots, stripes, coral, mitosis) seen in nature. The user picks from named parameter presets (e.g., "coral growth", "cell division", "fingerprints", "worms") and watches the pattern self-organize in real-time with colored ASCII shading. This fills a notable gap — the project has 94 modes covering fractals, fluids, particles, and cellular automata, but no classic reaction-diffusion system, which is one of the most visually striking simulations in computational science.
